pattern: Process deadlines in ascending order and greedily keep courses in a max-heap, dropping the longest one whenever the running time overflows a deadline.
hints: Sorting by deadline lets you consider courses in the order their windows close.
Maintain the set of chosen courses and the total time spent; if adding a course pushes total past its deadline, you may be able to swap out a longer already-chosen course.
Sort by lastDay. Keep a max-heap of chosen durations and a running sum. Add each course; if sum exceeds its deadline, pop the largest duration. The heap size is the answer.

edge_inputs.description: single course that cannot finish in time
first statement example, take three of four
third statement example, none can be taken
swapping a long early course for room enables more later courses
all same deadline, only the shortest combination fits
chain of courses each fitting exactly

stress.description: many random courses with bounded duration and deadline
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(1, size)
    courses = []
    for _ in range(n):
        dur = rng.randint(1, 200)
        last = rng.randint(1, 4000)
        courses.append([dur, last])
    return [courses]
stress.note: deadlines and durations stay within bounds; tests the heap-swap greedy
